name: Augment Boarding Party
description long: A boarding party of genetically modified [[Augment]]s beams aboard an enemy vessel, disabling its shields and attempting to seize it. If the ship would be destroyed, instead it warps out, becoming available to the Captain as an Admiralty Card. Up to one such card per eligible ship class may be held'"`UNIQ--ref-00000002-QINU`"' at a time, and the Cards are consumed on use. Eligible classes are [[:Category:Frigates (npc)|Frigate]], [[:Category:Cruisers (npc)|Cruiser]], [[:Category:Battleships (npc)|Battleship]], and [[:Category:Dreadnoughts (npc)|Dreadnought]]. The use of Augments in modern days is rare but extant. Prohibited by almost all space-faring races after past attempts, small populations of Augments persist in holding cells and back alleys across the galaxy. They are all too willing to assist in combat in exchange for certain liberties.

Auxiliary Power to the Inertial Dampers (Aux2Damp) redirects auxiliary power to provide significant kinetic damage resistance, movement turn and speed improvement, and immunity to repel and disable effects.

:It is a fast cycling general purpose mobility and defense ability. When combined with [[Evasive Maneuvers (ability)|Evasive Maneuvers]], it is possible to exceed full impulse speeds even during active combat. The immunity to repel and disable effects will nullify the push/pull effects of abilities such as [[Tractor Beam Repulsors (ability)|Tractor Beam Repulsors]] and [[Gravity Well (ability)|Gravity Well]], but it will not directly counter [[Tractor Beam (ability)|Tractor Beam]]s, although the extra speed may still facilitate an escape.

name: Beams: Fire at Will
description: Attack Upgrade - Multi-Target Rate of Fire
description long: Beams: Fire at Will (FaW) improves your beam weapons, allowing them to fire at multiple targets of their choice, for a short duration. This ability applies to both [[Beam Array]]s and [[Dual Beam Bank]]s. A standard beam attack cycle is four attacks against a single target. After activation, ''all'' beam weapons will attack the selected target (if within its arc, otherwise a random target) and a second random target within their firing arc and attack them five times each (for a total of 10 beam attacks per cycle). The effect will apply to any firing cycle that begins during the ability's duration. If only one target is present, only five attacks are performed. This ability is effective in delivering sustained fire against a few ships or for taking out fighter swarms and targetable projectiles.

name: Beams: Overload
description long:
Beams: Overload (BO) improves your beam weapons by massively increasing their damage, for a short duration, at the cost of slower shots for the duration. This ability applies to both [[Beam Array]]s and [[Dual Beam Bank]]s. When activated, it can be used to quickly lower a shield facing, or to inflict significant burst damage to an exposed hull.  
:The ability's damage is a set multiple of the underlying weapon damage, with higher ranks providing a larger damage multiplier. Increasing your training in weapons skills will improve the underlying weapon damage, resulting in an increase in the damage of BO. With higher ranks of this ability, the proper tactical consoles, and significant weapons training, it is possible to inflict devastating amounts of damage to an exposed hull on a critical hit.  
:Having one BO buff active will prevent you from activating any other copies of BO. Consuming a BO buff to fire a BO will trigger a 5 second cooldown period during which no other BO powers can be activated. This 5 second cooldown period is not triggered if the buff just wears off due to duration/unuse, or if the buff is purged by a dispel effect.

name: Delayed Overload Cascade
description: Enemies in Target Area explode for Damage and Knockback
description long: Charges target enemy craft and those nearby it with destructive energy. After a brief delay, all afflicted enemies explode, taking massive damage, dealing half damage to other enemies near themselves, and knocking them away. If multiple enemies are charged, multiple explosions occur, making this weapon devastating against swarms. Max 8 targets.<br><br>Convergent evolution, as a principle, holds true even across timelines. After a disastrous combat against alien ships which swarmed like insects, Starfleet needed to rethink how they handled small adversaries. Where traditional fighter craft operate as a screen and striking arm of larger ships, these adversaries used small ships exclusively and in immense number.<br><br>In our timeline, the U.S.S. Voyager inspired certain advances. In another timeline, it was the U.S.S. Franklin. Their cure to this plague was quite innovative: a resonant energy field induces massive molecular detachment in tightly packed enemy craft, enabling this little doctor to prematurely end their game.

description long:
Extend Shields (ES) regenerates the shields of the target ally and improves their shield damage resistance. Increases in effectiveness based on current shield power level.
:*''Usage notes'': Be aware that the ability has a 10 km range. This means that if you apply it to an ally, and then they go beyond 10 Km, they stop benefiting from the heal. However, targets can sail back into the 10 Km sphere and regain the heal. Hence, it is important to corral your team-mates (in-game chat/voice, or 3rd party applications), as well as being mindful of your proximity to your team-mates too.
:**This is NOT an ability that you can apply to yourself. Therefore, if you <u>only</u> play solo content (such as story content and patrols), then this ability should be avoided.
:**ES is a channeled ability, so the 15 second cooldown on the ability does not start until the ability ends. Once the ability is activated, it cannot be manually cancelled early by the player, nor can the target of the ability be changed.

description long:
Gives all your subsystems a large increase in power and maximum power for a duration, at the cost of a subsystem going offline briefly afterwards.

:Temporarily override the safety measures in place for all power subsystems, allowing them to support far more power for a limited amount of time. This power dump will decay each second while the safety is overridden, meaning that the benefit you gain will also gradually decrease over the power's duration. When the power bonus ends, the strain caused by this procedure will cause one random subsystem to go offline for a short duration. description long: Stealth Module masks you from sight, but enemies can detect you at close range. While cloaked, your abilities that affect others (including attacks) will remove the cloak.<br><br>Version I has a 3% variance in its stealth strength, Version II has a 2% variance, and Version III has a 1% variance.

name: Strategic Analysis
description long: By actively analyzing the battlefield during combat, you can gradually improve the effectiveness of yourself and nearby allies. These bonuses will grow in intensity over the course of combat. You may only activate this ability while in combat.

name: Structural Analysis
description long: Structural Analysis rapidly finds weaknesses within a target ship, reducing their damage resistance for a short time. This does not require a significant amount of processing power; while Structural Analysis is running, it can identify weaknesses within other nearby targets concurrently. Any given target may only be affected by one instance of Structural Analysis at a given time.

This gas will slow and weaken any nearby foes, and has a chance to put them into a coughing fit that prevents all other actions. You may have up to four of these mines deployed at once. Note: Enemies that don't breathe, or are wearing Environmental Suits or Rebreathers, are not affected by Anesthizine Gas!

This sudden transport also makes you temporarily more difficult to target by enemy weaponry.<br><br>This ability will always be used on the nearest ally within 50m, but may only be activated if they are below 50% Health. The duration of the Untargetable effect scales up, the lower their Health % is at the time of activation. If an Ally is made Untargetable by this ability, they cannot benefit from this ability for several seconds.

name: Torpedoes: High Yield
description long:
Torpedoes: High Yield (T:HY, HYT) upgrades the next [[Torpedo Launcher|torpedo]] attack to fire a salvo of torpedoes at a single target.  Each rank will fire one additional torpedo in the salvo (Rank 1 fires two torpedoes, Rank 2 fires three, and Rank 3 fires four).  Some torpedoes (all [[Tricobalt Torpedo Launcher|Tricobalt-based]], some Reputation/Lobi, and all [[Plasma Torpedo Launcher|Plasma Torpedos]]) will fire a single, highly destructive, 'heavy' torpedo.  With the exception of the Enhanced Bio-Molecular Photon Torpedo, all 'heavy' torpedoes (denoted as 'heavies') move significantly slower than normal torpedoes, and can be [[Destructible torpedo|destroyed]] in mid-flight.

:In most cases, torpedoes that have a percent chance to generate a secondary effect will have a 100% chance to generate said secondary effect under this firing mode (e.g. [[Gravimetric Photon Torpedo Launcher|Gravimetric Photon Torpedo]]).
